timeline.summary: Learned: Intermittent facial grimacing, frequent throat clearing, and occasional hand movements require evaluation for Tourette Syndrome. This will improve future anxiety disorder diagnoses.

details.principle_text: Intermittent facial grimacing, frequent throat clearing, and occasional hand movements require evaluation for Tourette Syndrome. This can prevent misdiagnosis and inappropriate treatment of anxiety disorders.
details.reasoning: The symptoms of Tourette Syndrome can be confused with anxiety, leading to incorrect diagnoses; recognizing the motor and vocal tics is critical for accurate assessment.
details.viewer_output: Learned: Intermittent facial grimacing, frequent throat clearing, and occasional hand movements require evaluation for Tourette Syndrome. This will improve future anxiety disorder diagnoses.
